preamble: The voiceless retroflex affricate [ʈʂ] can undergo a phonetic transformation to the voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ], a shift observed in certain varieties of Mandarin Chinese.
phoneticEffects: This transformation involves both a change in voicing, from voiceless to voiced, and a shift in the place of articulation from retroflex to postalveolar

examples.note: Retroflex affricates in Standard Mandarin (like those represented by pinyin 'zh') are realized as postalveolar affricates in some varieties of Taiwanese Mandarin.

sources: A Case Study of Phonological Attrition of Taiwanese Mandarin in California - eScholarship.org
